IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: We are upgrading a piece of equipment called a transformer, following a customer request for a new electricity supply. Once complete this work will adhere to the customer request as we have a duty to provide electricity when requested. This will also ensure our network can uphold the increased electricity usage in your area. This work will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate you may have been impacted by unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. To complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off and upgrade the transformer. We will remove the existing one and install a new one on the same day. This work is taking place in the Church Lane area. A road closure will be in place to allow the works to be carried out safely. Access to your property will always be provided.

This work is taking place on Rookery Close. The W3W for this location is: tests.gossiping.tickles.

Sent by David at 22:47 - Hi David here again. I'm pleased to confirm that all power in the Tariff Road N17 0 area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fault on the underground electrical network which our engineers have repaired.

We know that a power cut can be difficult, and I'm sorry for the length of the time you were without your electricity. Please rest assured that our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hi David here from the messaging team. I'm so sorry that power is still off this evening. Just to let you know. Our engineers have just called in to advise us they have run into complications with the ongoing repairs to the faulty underground electrical equipment which is hindering up restoring power 

Unfortunately, it's going to take us past our current time frame to complete this safely, so we're now working towards between 22:30-23:30 to have power back on

We can appreciate how frustrating and disruptive being without power can be, and our staff are working as fast as they can. If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to speak to our team, but I'll let you know further news as it comes in from site. Sent by David at 22:23.

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.
